entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: An important rainfed pulse crop of tropics and sub-tropics.

entities.name: TROPICS AND SUB-TROPICS
entities.description: Geographic regions where pigeonpea is cultivated.

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Biological stresses encountered by pigeonpea during its growth cycle.

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Non-biological stresses encountered by pigeonpea during its growth cycle.

entities.name: CMS
entities.description: Cytoplasmic Male Sterility system used to develop hybrids.

entities.name: CMS-BASED PIGEONPEA HYBRIDS
entities.description: Pigeonpea hybrids developed using the CMS system.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced, showing gains in hybrids.

entities.name: STABILITY
entities.description: The consistency of crop performance, showing gains in hybrids.

entities.name: INBRED CULTIVARS
entities.description: Traditional pure line varieties of pigeonpea.

entities.name: HETEROSIS
entities.description: The superior performance of hybrids over their parents, expressed in various traits.

entities.name: SEED GERMINATION
entities.description: A trait where heterosis is expressed in hybrid plants.

entities.name: RADICLE GROWTH
entities.description: A trait where heterosis is expressed in hybrid plants.

entities.name: ROOT BIOMASS PRODUCTION
entities.description: A trait where heterosis is expressed in hybrid plants.

entities.name: MOISTURE RETENTION
entities.description: A trait where heterosis is expressed in hybrid plants during water stress.

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: A specific abiotic stress affecting moisture retention.

entities.name: PURE LINE CULTIVARS
entities.description: Cultivars derived from self-pollinated lines, compared to hybrids.
